mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate signals a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer confidence and consistent corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of observations when UNRATE is below 4.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical threshold, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, thereby negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sustained growth in retail sales and a declining unemployment rate indicates robust consumer demand and a tightening labor market. This environment typically translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising retail sales and falling unemployment does not lead to statistically significant positive SPX 20d returns (p-value > 0.1) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a significant decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply side of the economy without alleviating price pressures. This scenario leads to stagflationary concerns, negatively impacting corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO declines significantly while CPIAUCSL YoY is above 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts indicates robust activity in the construction sector and strong consumer confidence in the economy. This typically precedes broader economic growth and translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 35% of periods following a sustained surge in housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes consumer purchasing power and compresses corporate profit margins, often prompting central banks to implement tighter monetary policies. This combination collectively leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL YoY is above 3.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a strong and expanding labor market, which directly increases household income and consumption. This supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 45% of instances where PAYEMS shows strong monthly growth exceeding 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Strong reported GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, providing a favorable macroeconomic environment for corporate profits and equity valuations. This fundamental strength leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 30% of periods following strong reported GDP growth (QoQ annualized > 2.0%) for two consecutive quarters.
